title: rosa glutinosa subsp. pustulosa
intro: Rosa glutinosa subsp. pustulosa, also known as the sticky rose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, characterized by its fragrant, pink flowers and sticky leaves. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal medicine, with medicinal properties attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Rosa glutinosa subsp. pustulosa include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a valuable remedy in traditional Chinese medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and teas, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
